LAW AND MOTION, 302, LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SALESFORCE.COM, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON SEP-23-2019 BY ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST TO VACATE/SET ASIDE THE DISMISSAL IS ALSO TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION PENDING A FILING OF SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS THIS FRIDAY. BOTH SIDES TO FILE SIMULTANEOUS BRIEF BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON FRIDAY, 9/27/19, ON THE LIMITED ISSUE OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF MAY AVOID A COURT'S RULING BY VOLUNTARILY FILING A DISMISSAL AFTER THE COURT HAS ISSUED A TENTATIVE RULING THAT IS ADVERSE TO THEM. COUNSEL FOR SALEFORCE IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING IN WORD FORMAT BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 63151312) FILED BY PLAINTIFF JANE DOES #1 THROUGH #50,SEX TRAFFICKING SURVIVORS AS TO DEFENDANT SALESFORCE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION AND A CALIFORNIA CITIZEN
DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 63538388) FILED BY DEFENDANT SALESFORCE.COM, INC. HEARING SET FOR AUG-22-2019 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SALESFORCE.COM, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFFS SUBMITTED A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR FILING ON AUGUST 2, 2019. (SEE STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 184 CALAPP.4TH 1124, 1131 ["THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT MOOTS A MOTION DIRECTED TO A PRIOR COMPLAINT."].) IF DEFENDANTS DO NOT BELIEVE THE AMENDMENTS OVERCOME THE DEFECTS IN THE PRIOR COMPLAINT, THEY MUST MEET AND CONFER AGAIN WITH PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL AND THEN, IF UNABLE TO RESOLVE THE ISSUES, FILE A NEW DEMURRER. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. SEC. 430.41 ["IF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT . . . IS FILED, THE RESPONDING PARTY SHALL MEET AND CONFER AGAIN WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE AMENDED PLEADING BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED PLEADING."].) IN BRIEFING ON ANY SUBSEQUENT DEMURRER, THE PARTIES SHOULD ADDRESS DYROFF V. THE ULTIMATE SOFTWARE GROUP, INC., NO. 18-15175 (9TH CIR. AUG. 20, 2019). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Appendix Containing Plaintiffs Identifying Information; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Victoria L. Weatherford, Esq. of GIBSON DUNN & CRUTCHER LLP, T:415.393.8200, for defendant/ moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SALESFORCE.COM, INC.'s Motion To Seal Appendix Containing Plaintiffs Identifying Information is Granted. No opposition filed, and good cause shown pursuant to California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551.

The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sharon Arkin, Esq. 541-469-7892 of the ARkin Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Kristin Linsley, Esq. and Matthew Kahn, Esq. 415-393-8200, Christopher Jones, Esq. 303-319-6055 of Gibson Dunn for Defendant Salesforce.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court informs parties it has received a copy of Plaintiff's request for dismissal which is pending on the Court's efiling system on 9/22/19, shares its views, and cites case law. Plaintiff requests with leave to amend if the Court will stand by its tentative ruling. Defendant argues, cites cases and orally requests to vacate/set aside the dismissal. Both sides argue the Demurrer. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SALESFORCE.COM, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Demurrer is Taken Under submission. Plaintiff's oral request to vacate/set aside the Dismissal is also Taken Under Submission pending a filing of Supplemental Briefs this Friday. Both sides to file simultaneous brief by the close of business on Friday, 9/27/19, on the limited issue of whether Plaintiff may avoid a Court's ruling by voluntarily filing a dismissal after the Court has issued a tentative ruling that is adverse to them.

Counsel for Saleforce is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's tentative ruling in word format by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT SALESFORCE.COM, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiffs submitted a second amended complaint for filing on August 2, 2019. (See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 CalApp.4th 1124, 1131 ["the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint."].) If defendants do not believe the amendments overcome the defects in the prior complaint, they must meet and confer again with plaintiffs' counsel and then, if unable to resolve the issues, file a new demurrer. (See Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 430.41 ["If an amended complaint . . . is filed, the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a demurrer to the amended pleading."].) In briefing on any subsequent demurrer, the parties should address Dyroff v. The Ultimate Software Group, Inc., No. 18-15175 (9th Cir. Aug. 20, 2019).

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
